retention.answer: The status line fetches account usage from `https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage` via a GET request with an OAuth bearer token.

The per-session stdin rate_limits snapshot was rejected because it was frozen at each session's start time, causing different sessions to display different 5h/7d percentages; the shared cache ensures all sessions show identical account-glob
The command `ps -A` was found to take seconds on a busy process table and was avoided in favor of targeted per-pid queries like `ps -p PID` which run in ~10ms.
In the responsive layout, detail is dropped in this priority order as the pane narrows: model name → context window → countdowns → bars → 5h/7d labels, with the percentages always surviving.
The status-line process has no tty of its own—its stdin/stdout are pipes—so it must read the winsize of its parent process's (Claude Code's) controlling tty to determine the actual terminal width.
The function is named `visible_len`, which strips ANSI escape sequences and returns the visible character count of a string.

The two hook events are **UserPromptSubmit** (runs `inject-date.sh` to inject the date at the start of every turn) and **PostToolUse** (runs `inject-time.sh` to refresh the time during long tasks, throttled to once per minute).
The default throttle interval is 60 seconds, overridden by the `CC_TIME_INTERVAL` environment variable (see `inject-time.sh`: `INTERVAL="${CC_TIME_INTERVAL:-60}"`).
The dark 'pill' background RGB color is (22, 23, 30), defined in statusline.py as the "dark island" shown when THEME == "pill".
The default theme is "pill," which renders a bright neon palette on a dark background that lets the neon colors pop against a cream terminal.

At high utilization (position 1.0), the gradient bar reaches dark blood red (140, 16, 22) in light theme or bright red (255, 60, 60) in dark theme, as defined in the `STOPS_LIGHT` and `STOPS_DARK` color palettes.
All sessions display the same numbers simultaneously, rather than disagreeing across sessions with stale per-session snapshots.

The provided code shows that usage comes from either a cached source (`cached_usage()`) or stdin payload (`usage_from_payload()`), but the `cached_usage()` function implementation—which would contain any HTTP endpoint detail
Because the rate limits are account-global—the same numbers in every session—so a shared cache provides one authoritative source rather than redundant per-session copies.
`ps -A` (the whole process table) was found to take seconds on a busy machine and had to be avoided in favor of targeted per-pid queries with `ps -p PID`.
Detail is shed in priority order: model name → context window → countdowns → bars → 5h/7d labels, with percentages surviving longest.
The status-line process has no tty of its own because its stdin/stdout are pipes, so it must read an ancestor process's tty to access the terminal width.
The function is `visible_len`, which strips ANSI escape sequences and returns the on-screen character count.

The two hook events are **UserPromptSubmit** (which runs `inject-date.sh` to inject the date at the start of every turn) and **PostToolUse** (which runs `inject-time.sh` to refresh the time during long tasks, throttled to ~once per minute).
The default throttle interval for the time-injection hook is 60 seconds, overridden by the `CC_TIME_INTERVAL` environment variable.

Shared cache ensures all Claude Code sessions show identical rate-limit state at any moment, whereas per-session stdin snapshots would diverge and create inconsistent displays across parallel sessions.
`ps -A` was found to be slow (avoided in favor of targeted `ps -p PID` queries, which take ~10ms).
Detail is dropped in this priority order: model → context window → countdowns → bars → labels, with percentages always remaining.

The context describes *how* the statusline finds width (walks parent process chain, finds Claude Code's controlling pty, reads via TIOCGWINSZ), but doesn't explicitly state *why* it must read an ancestor's tty rather than it
The function is `visible_len()`, which measures on-screen width by ignoring ANSI color codes.

UserPromptSubmit runs `inject-date.sh` to inject the current date at the start of every turn, and PostToolUse runs `inject-time.sh` to refresh the time during long tasks (throttled to ~once per minute).
The default throttle interval is 60 seconds, overridden by the `CC_TIME_INTERVAL` environment variable.

Shared cache sourcing ensures **all sessions display identical usage statistics** ("Shared cache (not stdin) is source of truth—all sessions identical").
`CC_CACHE_TTL` controls the cache refresh frequency in seconds (default 15s); set it to 1 to run every second.

NOT IN CONTEXT

The provided context includes the installation scripts and optional time-hook features, but not the `statusline.py` implementation details that would explain the ANSI reset handling for the pill's background color.
The status line fetches account usage from the Anthropic OAuth endpoint `https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage` via a GET request with a Bearer token authorization header.

The stdin rate_limits payload is a per-session snapshot frozen at that session's last API call, which made the limits disagree across sessions—the shared cache ensures every session displays the same account-global numbers.
The `ps -A` command was found to take seconds on a busy process table and had to be avoided in favor of a targeted ps walk.
In the responsive layout, detail is dropped in this priority order as the pane narrows: model name → ctx% → reset countdowns → bars → 5h/7d labels, ensuring the percentages always survive.
The status-line process has no tty of its own—its stdin/stdout are pipes—so it must read the controlling tty from an ancestor process (Claude Code, its parent) to get the live pane width.
The function is `visible_len`, which strips ANSI escape sequences and counts the visible character width of a string.

The two hook events are **UserPromptSubmit** (runs `hooks/inject-date.sh` to inject the date at the start of each turn) and **PostToolUse** (runs `hooks/inject-time.sh` to refresh the time during long tasks, throttled to one injection per 6
The default throttle interval for the time-injection hook is 60 seconds, overridden by the `CC_TIME_INTERVAL` environment variable.
The dark 'pill' background is RGB (22, 23, 30), defined in `statusline.py` as `BG_FILL = (22, 23, 30) if THEME == "pill" else None`.
The default theme is "pill," which uses a bright neon palette on a dark background pill to make the neon colors pop on a cream-colored terminal.
At high utilization near the limit, the gradient bar reaches red—dark blood red for the light theme or bright red (#FF3C3C) for the dark theme.
Sourcing usage from one shared cache file ensures that every session shows identical numbers, rather than having each session display its own per-session snapshot that would disagree across sessions.
NOT IN CONTEXT

The `with_background()` function re-arms the background color code after every ANSI reset by replacing `\033[0m` with `\033[0m` + `BGCODE`, ensuring the pill background survives foreground-only resets.
